title: competitive-analyst output
description: Output of the f04 competitive-analyst agent (gpt-5.6-terra). Each competitors[] element is written verbatim as the value of one competition.competitor claim on the idea_vs_market axis (design.md 3.2/3.3). threat_level and switching_cost are OUR OWN rubrics and may be null; null is a first-class value contributing exactly 0 to design.md 6.4, never a penalty. The agent emits no mismatch severity, no Trust number and no axis score — those are computed deterministically downstream (design.md 8).

code.description: Finite set; the agent may not invent codes. 'abstained' is a COMPLETED job (design.md 9).

ok.description: true for both 'success' and 'abstained'; false for 'insufficient_input' and 'processing_error'. CONSUMER: the n8n IF node routing to the gap-claim writer vs the error branch (design.md 4).

founder_view_assessable.description: false when founder_competition_view_present was false — a deckless radar_activated entry where the founder was never asked about competition. CONSUMER: design.md 6.4 gates the -10 'named zero competitors' term on this, and design.md 8's severity ladder ends 'no deck / no founder competition view -> no mismatch claim at all (not assessable)'. false also raises missing_flags.founder_competition_view_absent.

competitors.description: CONSUMER: one competition.competitor claim per element (design.md 3.2), value = per_competitor_record (design.md 3.3), item_key = normalised competitor name (design.md 3.5). An empty array is valid ONLY alongside bucket_coverage values and a gaps[] entry — never silently.

category.description: per_competitor_record vocabulary, borrowed verbatim from reporting (Apache-2.0) per design.md 3.3. CONSUMER: the adjacent_competitors / incumbent_competitors buckets (design.md 8) and the memo's named clusters (feature 06).

company_mentioned.description: Did the founder name them? CONSUMER: the deterministic mismatch node (design.md 8) — found \ named produces the competition.founder_claim_mismatch claim on the trust axis AND a relation='contradicts' evidence row on the founder's competition claim. Also the company_unnamed_competitors bucket, which design.md 8 calls the HIGHEST-VALUE OUTPUT. Always false when founder_view_assessable is false.

positioning_summary.description: 1-2 NEUTRAL sentences. CONSUMER: memo Competition section (feature 06), dashboard competitor table (feature 09).

stage.description: per_competitor_record vocabulary (design.md 3.3). 'unknown' unless a document states otherwise — never inferred from website polish.

most_recent_funding.description: design.md 3.3 shape {round, amount, date, lead}, EXTENDED with source_url because design.md 9 requires every numeric assertion to carry the source_url it came from and 'amount' is a number. CONSUMERS: memo Competition section (feature 06); funding recency colours design.md 8's severity ladder ('>=3 found unnamed incl. funded -> moderate').

amount.description: As stated by the source (e.g. '$100M'). MUST be null unless source_url is non-null — a number whose source cannot be pointed at must not be emitted at all.

differentiation_vs_target.description: Neutrally framed, grounded in the documents (design.md 3.3). CONSUMER: memo Competition section — differentiation (feature 06).

source_urls.description: Every document URL supporting this record. minItems 1 is the structural guarantee that no competitor is recalled from training data rather than found in the input.

threat_level.description: OUR OWN RUBRIC (design.md 3.3), seeded by JTBD and NEVER to be attributed to Porter/SWOT/Blue Ocean. 1 = disruptive innovation (lower-level jobs eliminated) · 2 = upstream interception · 3 = blue-ocean niche (copyable by a large incumbent) · 4 = perfect competition / red ocean, a pre-seed red flag. CONSUMERS: design.md 6.4 term (1 -> +15, 2 -> +8, 3 -> 0, 4 -> -20, null -> 0); dashboard competitor table sort (feature 09); PostgREST query value->>'threat_level' (feature 10). null is a FIRST-CLASS value when evidence does not support a reading and contributes exactly 0, never a penalty. Range-checked deterministically after emission.

threat_level_rationale.description: REQUIRED (non-null) whenever threat_level is non-null; MUST be null when threat_level is null. design.md 3.3: the ordinals are LLM-proposed WITH CITED EVIDENCE, then range-checked. No rationale means no reading.

switching_cost.description: The 10x heuristic (design.md 3.3 — this rubric IS sourced from the corpus, unlike threat_level). 1 = verifiably 10x cheaper/faster/more effective, the switch happens · 2 = 10-20% better, 'such a switch very rarely happens' · 3 = feature parity, acquisition requires pure marketing spend. CONSUMERS: design.md 6.4 term (1 -> +20, 2 -> 0, 3 -> -15, null -> 0) — the largest single weight on the axis; and the third condition of design.md 7's underestimation guard, which fires only when switching_cost = 1. A founder's own '10x faster' claim is NOT verification. null when unsupported.

switching_cost_rationale.description: REQUIRED (non-null) whenever switching_cost is non-null; MUST be null when switching_cost is null. Must name the benchmark, published price gap or figures-bearing case study that supports the reading.

bucket_coverage.description: design.md 8's five required output buckets, each affirmatively addressed. Emitted as coverage states rather than as duplicate name lists so the records live in exactly one place and cannot drift; the deterministic node derives membership from competitors[].category and competitors[].company_mentioned. 'searched_none_found' is a FINDING; 'not_searched' is a HOLE and must be explained in gaps[]. CONSUMER: the *.gap claim writer and the memo's coverage statement (feature 06).

status_quo_alternatives.description: CONSUMER: competition.status_quo_alternative claims (design.md 3.2). 'Sometimes a spreadsheet is the real competitor.'

alternative.description: design.md 3.2 value key. What the buyer does today instead.

displaced.description: EXTENSION of design.md 3.2's listed value shape, required by design.md 6.4: 'Displaced is a stricter predicate than identified and the two must not be conflated'. true ONLY with a cited source showing the product REMOVES the manual work the alternative requires. CONSUMERS: design.md 6.4's +7 term keys on DISPLACED; design.md 7's underestimation guard keys on IDENTIFIED (i.e. on the presence of any element in this array), which is why the two predicates must stay separable.

displacement_source_url.description: REQUIRED (non-null) whenever displaced is true; null otherwise.

accumulating_advantage.description: CONSUMER: design.md 6.4's 'articulated accumulating advantage w/ public evidence' term — present -> +10, or +8 when threat_level = 1 AND switching_cost = 1 (the deliberate nonlinearity holding the axis maximum at exactly 100); absent or unknown -> 0. ADDITIVE ONLY: an absent moat costs NOTHING, because demanding proven network effects at pre-seed is 'way too strict a bar' and generates the false negatives this product exists to prevent. Raw build speed qualifies as nothing (SIG-013).

kind.description: The evidenceable pre-seed moats enumerated in design.md 6.4. Non-null iff present is true.

public_artifact_url.description: REQUIRED (non-null) whenever present is true — design.md 6.4 requires the advantage to be 'backed by a public artifact'.

evidence.description: CONSUMER: evidence rows (design.md 3.4/3.5). tier and strength are assigned by the validator from the deterministic domain map, never by this agent.

supports.description: Dotted path to the field this row supports, e.g. 'competitors[1].most_recent_funding.amount', 'competitors[0].switching_cost', 'status_quo_alternatives[0]', 'accumulating_advantage'. CONSUMER: the write node uses it to attach the evidence row to the correct claim.

source_url.description: null ONLY on a relation='context' row recording that a search ran and returned nothing (design.md 3.5: tier='missing' rows use relation='context' with NULL url and NULL quote).

quote_verbatim.description: The source's own sentence, WORD FOR WORD, unedited and untranslated — the RSK-003 verbatim layer against LLM re-centering (design.md 2).

relation.description: Matches the evidence table's relation column. 'context' rows are EXCLUDED from the confidence evidence count (design.md 6.5).

gaps.description: MANDATORY explicit gap declaration — the deliberate inversion of company-research-agent's 'Never mention no information found' (design.md 9). CONSUMERS: *.gap claims with verification_status='missing' (design.md 3.2), scores.missing_flags (design.md 3.7), missing_count in the confidence formula (design.md 6.5).

field.description: Dotted path or top-level field name, e.g. 'competitors[0].switching_cost', 'status_quo_alternatives', 'founder_view_assessable'.

reason_code.description: Closed vocabulary so the validator maps gaps onto missing_flags deterministically. 'founder_view_absent' specifically raises missing_flags.founder_competition_view_absent and zeroes the design.md 6.4 -10 term.

coverage.description: 'not_applicable' is the correct value for company_named_competitors when founder_view_assessable is false.

competitors.name: FloQast
competitors.positioning_summary: Established accounting close-management platform serving mid-market and enterprise finance teams.
competitors.differentiation_vs_target: Far broader product and enterprise sales motion; documents show no community-bank-specific regulatory reporting.
competitors.threat_level_rationale: A well-capitalised incumbent that could move down-market into community banks; the niche is real but copyable.

status_quo_alternatives.alternative: Excel reconciliation with results e-mailed between the controller and branch staff.
status_quo_alternatives.why_it_wins_today: Already licensed, requires no procurement approval, and the controller already knows it.

evidence.quote_verbatim: FloQast, which automates the accounting close, said Tuesday it had raised $100 million in a Series E led by ICONIQ Growth.

gaps.note: No benchmark, published price comparison or case study with figures was found.

status.message: One possible competitor found with insufficient detail to assess; no founder competition view exists (deckless entry).

competitors.name: Akvakontroll
competitors.positioning_summary: Listed in a Norwegian aquaculture supplier directory as providing compliance reporting; no product detail available in the retrieved pages.
competitors.differentiation_vs_target: Cannot be assessed — the directory entry contains no feature or pricing information.

gaps.note: Radar-activated entry with no deck or interview; the founder has never been asked about competition, so nothing can be concluded from their silence.
A directory listing with no product detail cannot support any of the four criteria.
No document describes how operators handle discharge reporting today.
